root_text.an9.72:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho.
root_text.an9.72:1.4: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an9.72:1.5: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, ayaṁ paṭhamo cetasovinibandho.
root_text.an9.72:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye avītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an9.72:2.3: yāvadatthaṁ udarāvadehakaṁ bhuñjitvā seyyasukhaṁ passasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ anuyutto viharati …
root_text.an9.72:2.4: aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti.
root_text.an9.72:2.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an9.72:2.6: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, ayaṁ pañcamo cetasovinibandho.
root_text.an9.72:3.1: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an9.72:3.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.an9.72:3.6: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.an9.72:3.7: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya ime cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an9.72:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.72:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant isn’t free of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an9.72:1.4: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an9.72:1.5: This is the first shackle of the heart.
translation_text.an9.72:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant isn’t free of greed for the body …
translation_text.an9.72:2.2: They’re not free of greed for form …
translation_text.an9.72:2.3: They eat as much as they like until their belly is full, then indulge in the pleasures of sleeping, lying down, and drowsing …
translation_text.an9.72:2.4: They lead the spiritual life wishing to be reborn in one of the orders of gods: ‘By this precept or observance or fervent austerity or spiritual practice, may I become one of the gods!’
translation_text.an9.72:2.5: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an9.72:2.6: This is the fifth shackle of the heart.
translation_text.an9.72:2.7: These are the five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.72:3.1: To give up these five shackles of the heart you should develop the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an9.72:3.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.an9.72:3.4: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.an9.72:3.5: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind …
translation_text.an9.72:3.6: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.an9.72:3.7: To give up these five shackles of the heart you should develop these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.”
